name: Web Positioning Conformance Demo
summary: The runnable proof of the PositioningStrategy contract (#1048) in a real browser: a surface declares intent — a Placement (bottom-start, top-end, …) plus collision flags (flip/shift/resize) — and a swappable strategy realizes it and returns a reversible PositioningTeardown. The contract is type-only (positioning/contract.ts); the native CSS-anchor strategy, JS fallback, and customPositioning swap registry are impl in Frontier UI, so the demo supplies its own in-demo JS placement strategy to prove the contract is realizable. A runtime-conformance section asserts each invariant live: place() returns a teardown, the strategy name reflects on data-positioning-strategy, a bottom placement sits below its trigger, an overflowing side flips, shift clamps within the viewport, and teardown is fully reversible.
description:
Concept

The runnable proof of the PositioningStrategy contract (#1048). A surface declares intent — a Placement plus collision flags (flip/shift/resize) — and a swappable strategy realizes it and returns a reversible PositioningTeardown.

In-demo strategy, not the FUI impl

The contract is type-only (positioning/contract.ts); the native CSS-anchor strategy, the JS fallback, and the customPositioning swap registry are impl and live in Frontier UI. So this demo supplies its own in-demo JS placement strategy — honest for a browser demo — to prove the contract is realizable.

Verified contract

The runtime-conformance section asserts each invariant live: place() returns a PositioningTeardown, the strategy name reflects on data-positioning-strategy, a bottom placement sits below its trigger, a side that overflows the viewport flips, shift clamps the surface within the viewport, and the teardown is fully reversible.
